headline: US Shale Drillers Seen Lifting Crude Output on Hormuz-Driven Price Rally
summary: (Bloomberg) -- US shale drillers are expected to follow President Donald Trump’s call for higher oil production — but not just because he said so. The 72% surge in crude prices since the US and Israel commenced attacks on Iran roughly five weeks ago is incentive enough to compel American oil executives to ramp up output, according to observers as diverse as Citigroup Inc. and Enverus Inc.Shale explorers require oil prices somewhere between $62 and $70 a barrel to turn a profit on new wells, acco

headline: U.S. Bank Stocks Follow The Broader Market Down In March
summary: Market uncertainty, exacerbated by the Middle East war, led to a widespread downturn in equities during March. Financial stocks, particularly banks, held up better than most other sectors.

headline: How The Herbalife (HLF) Story Is Shifting As Analysts Rethink Turnaround Potential
summary: Herbalife’s updated fair value estimate has shifted from US$14.00 to US$16.50, putting a fresh spotlight on what analysts think the shares might be worth. That change sits between a wider spread of views, with targets running from US$9 to US$21 as some analysts focus on a potential turnaround story while others stay cautious on fundamentals and sector pressures. As you read on, you will see how this evolving narrative could shape the way you track Herbalife from here. Analyst Price Targets...

headline: Samsung Beats High Estimates After AI Chip Sales Defy War Fears
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Samsung Electronics Co. earned a far stronger-than-expected eight-fold leap in quarterly profit, underscoring robust demand for AI memory chips in the face of markets roiled by war in the Middle East. Customers led by cloud service providers are ramping up orders for high-bandwidth memory and other chips used in data centers to feed artificial intelligence services, lifting both volumes and margins at the chips-to-smartphones conglomerate.Shares of Samsung, which have slumped from
